summary: Phase 2 frontend testing complete. All features working after fixing critical Fabric.js canvas initialization bug (React 19 StrictMode + loading state race condition). SpreadBlockEditor, FieldDefinitions tab, and template navigation all tested and verified.

bug_fixed.issue: Fabric.js canvas was not initializing. `useEffect` with empty deps `[]` ran BEFORE `loading=false`, so `canvasRef.current` was null (canvas not in DOM during loading state). Fixed by adding `loading` to deps array and guarding: `if (loading) return;`
bug_fixed.rca: The canvas element is NOT rendered when loading=true (component returns loading spinner instead). The original useEffect([]) fired once before data was fetched, found canvasRef.current=null, and never ran again. Fix: useEffect([loading]) with early return when loading=true.

test_results.settings_modal_opens_with_3_tabs: PASS - Field Mapping, Field Definitions, Default Styling
test_results.spread_editor_header_elements: PASS - Back btn, template name, preview toggle, save btn
test_results.spread_editor_empty_state: PASS - No block selected + available tokens shown

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: useEffect cleanup with canvas.dispose() + fabricRef.current=null still runs when loading changes to false->true (if navigating away mid-load). This is edge case and acceptable.
Minor: Console warnings about 'alphabetical' not being a valid CanvasTextBaseline enum - this is a Fabric.js 5.x + Chromium compatibility warning, non-breaking
Minor: 'Saved 0 blocks for page 1' toast appears when save is called even with no blocks on canvas - expected behavior but could be confusing UX
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fabric.js canvas initialization bug was fixed in SpreadBlockEditor.js - useEffect now depends on [loading] state. All Phase 2 features tested and working. Test template ID: 414c7edb-e024-4791-be25-99f17f44d3e6
